svg.text: PUNJABI
HISTORY & CULTURAL CONTEXT
PUNJABI ORIGINS
Descended from Sanskrit via Shauraseni Prakrit and Apabhramsha
Punjab = "Panj" (five) + "Ab" (water) — Land of Five Rivers
Five rivers: Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, Sutlej
~800 CE: Early Punjabi emerges from Apabhramsha dialects
Guru Granth Sahib (1604): Sacred scripture in Gurmukhi
Sikh tradition: language, script and religion intertwined
Waris Shah (1766): Heer Ranjha — masterwork of Punjabi literature
PARTITION & DIVERGENCE (1947)
India-Pakistan partition split the Punjabi region in two
Pakistani Punjab: Shahmukhi script (Arabic-based), Urdu official
Indian Punjab: Gurmukhi script, state language since 1966
Same spoken language; two writing systems; political border
PUNJABI TODAY
~125M speakers; 10th most spoken language in the world
Diaspora: UK, Canada, USA — major communities worldwide
Bhangra music brings Punjabi language globally
SINDHI HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
SINDH: CRADLE OF CIVILIZATION
Indus Valley Civilization (3300-1300 BCE): Mohenjo-daro in Sindh
Sindhi script = undeciphered Indus script (4000+ inscriptions)
711 CE: Arab conquest of Sindh — Arabic influence on vocabulary
Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai (1689-1752): greatest Sindhi poet
Shah Jo Risalo: 30 surs (musical chapters) — Sindhi soul
PARTITION & DIASPORA
1947: Hindu Sindhis fled to India; Muslim Sindhis remained
Indian Sindhi: adopted Devanagari; scattered across cities
Pakistani Sindhi: Perso-Arabic script; centered in Sindh province
Unique: Sindhi Hindu diaspora maintained identity without homeland
SHARED RIVER HERITAGE
"Sindh" = ancient name of the Indus River (Sanskrit: Sindhu)
"Punjab" = land of the five rivers that feed the Indus
Both civilizations built on the same Indus watershed
Lahore to Hyderabad: the Indus corridor connecting both peoples
Sanskrit → Prakrits → Apabhramshas → Punjabi + Sindhi
PLATE 53d ALT · PUNJABI / SINDHI · HISTORY
